steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence is the last one: "Such analysis, in turn, can contribute to reports of exaggerated effects." The phrase 'in turn' signals a consequence flowing from what came before: biased analysis (caused by small sample sizes) leads to exaggerated reports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'summarizes a shift in understanding of Hippocampus guttulatus': no shift in understanding is described; the sentence adds a downstream consequence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'elaborates on a potential consequence of relying on a small sample size': traces the causal chain from small sample size to biased analysis to exaggerated reports. ✓
  • Choice C — 'emphasizes the magnitude of Faleiro's reported effect': the sentence warns about exaggeration, it does not amplify Faleiro's claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'counters Clements' objection': the sentence supports Clements' concern, not counters it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
